refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under defined network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: The adaptive protocol logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints in future FR refinements to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send status and packet loss metrics to NGHE Solution and other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains overall coordination but not detailed communication logic.
Physical vehicle module; limited role in communication protocol management.
Primary module for teleoperation communication; best containment and minimal diffusion.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication quality management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of module overload if Teleoperation also handles extensive error correction logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider splitting communication protocol logic if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by implementing adaptive communication to meet packet loss limits.

diffusion_risks: Some telemetry reporting overlaps with NGHE Solution module.
Potential complexity concentration in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Teleoperation module; diffusion risks are manageable with clear interface definitions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and network link performance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality management to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine latency-related FRs to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Define clear telemetry data ownership and interface contracts between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, refinement is needed to explicitly address latency trade-offs and telemetry interface ownership to reduce diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and clarify telemetry reporting requirements for better architectural clarity.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Potential module complexity and diffusion in Teleoperation.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss and latency under adaptive protocol.
Interface usage and telemetry data ownership clarity.
